Is a Big Album Dropping? You Might Want to Watch the Road.
Some times of the year, like New Year’s Eve, can be scary for drivers, because fatal crashes rise. But did you know that the risk also rises following the spring daylight saving time reset?

I am enthralled by research like that. Another day when crashes are more frequent, Tax Day, is near, and a study I found brings up an intriguing uptick, reminding us of the dangers of distracted driving.
The researchers studied a relatively new phenomenon: driving while streaming. They found that more fatal crashes occur on days when the biggest albums are released.
It’s not just the physical distraction of reaching for buttons. I learned that simulation research showed that simply listening to unfamiliar, high-energy music could impair driving performance.
The researchers told me that their goal is not to take music out of our cars and trucks, but to design systems where we can all enjoy it, without taking our eyes off the road.
